title: CodexHost.project_root's designed primary signal (payload cwd) is unreachable — no shared-core call site passes a payload — and when wired it returns the session cwd verbatim, not the repo root
evidence: CodexHost.project_root docstring: 'the hook payload's cwd … the harness's own signal' is leg 1. But _hooklib.project_root() delegates as `get_host().project_root()` with NO payload, and every entry script (pre-write, pre-bash, pre-edit, post-write-edit, session-start, taskwrite, migration/security-pass) calls the no-arg form — grep confirms zero call sites pass a payload. So on Codex the only harness-provided root signal is dead code and resolution silently rests on 'the hook process cwd equals the session cwd' (unverifiable Codex internals). Separately, leg 1 itself returns `cwd` verbatim when `os.path.isdir(cwd)` — for a session started in `<repo>/src`, that is `<repo>/src`, so `arbiter_active(root)` would read `<repo>/src/.codearbiter/CONTEXT.md`, find nothing, and every gate would go silently dormant the moment any call site starts passing the payload as designed.
impact: Today: root resolution on Codex depends entirely on process-cwd + git-toplevel assumptions the seam was explicitly built to avoid; a Codex hook spawned from a different cwd (or a nested-repo session cwd) writes state/logs into the wrong repo's .codearbiter with no signal. Latent: the first refactor that wires the documented payload leg turns every subdir-cwd Codex session into full silent dormancy (fail-open).
recommendation: Resolve payload cwd to its git toplevel inside CodexHost.project_root (payload cwd as the *starting point* for rev-parse, falling back to the cwd itself for non-git trees), and thread the payload through _hooklib.project_root(payload=None) from the entry scripts that have one.
acceptance_criteria: CodexHost.project_root({"cwd": "<repo>/subdir"}) returns <repo> when <repo> is a git toplevel
At least the PreToolUse entries pass their parsed payload into root resolution
